title: American Founding Symbols Coloring Adventure
description: A fun coloring activity that introduces young students to important symbols and ideas from America's founding period.
instructions: Color each part of the picture according to the suggestions or use your own creative colors! As you color, think about what each symbol means for our country.

content.image: A large outlined drawing showing several American founding symbols arranged in a friendly, cartoon-style scene. The Liberty Bell is in the center, with the American flag waving behind it. To the left is a bald eagle holding an olive branch and arrows. To the right is the Constitution document with 'We the People' visible at the top. At the bottom is a simple outline of Independence Hall.

elements.name: Liberty Bell
elements.description: Color the Liberty Bell brown. This bell rang to announce the signing of the Declaration of Independence.

elements.name: American Flag
elements.description: Color the stripes red and white, and the star section blue with white stars.

elements.name: Bald Eagle
elements.description: Color the eagle's body brown, but make its head and tail white to show it's our national bird!

elements.name: Constitution
elements.description: Color the Constitution paper a light yellow or tan to make it look old and important.

elements.name: Independence Hall
elements.description: Color Independence Hall with red bricks and white windows. This is where our country's founders met.

learningFacts: The Liberty Bell is a symbol of American independence.
The American flag has 13 stripes for the original colonies and 50 stars for our states.
The bald eagle represents strength and freedom.
The Constitution begins with 'We the People' because the government works for the citizens.
Independence Hall in Philadelphia is where both the Declaration of Independence and Constitution were debated and signed.
